verses.sanskrit: दर्शयित्वा रामाय सीतायाः प्रैमार्गने ।वाक्यम् अन्वर्थम् अर्थज्ञः कबंधः पुनः अब्रवीत् ॥३-७३-१॥
verses.text:
On showing a concept for questing after Seetha that context-sensitive Kabandha further spoke this sentence 
which is inclusive of right concept to Rama. [3-73-1]

verses.sanskrit: एष राम शिवः पंथा यत्र एते पुष्पिता द्रुमाः ।प्रतीचीम् दिशम् आश्रित्य प्रकाशन्ते मनो रमाः ॥३-७३-२॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama, have a recourse to westward, and where the trees of Rose-apple, Priyaala, Jackfruit, Plaksha, 
Banyan, Tinduka, Pipal, Karnikara, Mango, and others... and even trees like Dhanva, Naaga, Tilaka, Naktamaala, 
blue Ashoka, Kadamba, Karaviira, Agnimukha, Ashoka, red-sandalwood trees and Neem trees will be heart-pleasingly 
shining forth in full blossom, that alone is an optimistic course for you. [3-73-2, 3, 4, 5a]

verses.sanskrit: जंबू प्रियाल पनसाः प्लक्ष न्यग्रोध तिंदुकाः ।अश्वत्थाः कर्णिकाराः च चूताः च अन्ये च पादपाः ॥३-७३-३॥
धन्वना नाग वृक्षा तिलका नक्तमालकाः ।नील अशोक कदंबाः च करवीराः च पुष्पिताः ॥३-७३-४॥
अग्निमुखा अशोकाः च सुरक्ताः परिभद्रकाः ।तान् आरुह्य अथवा भूमौ पातयित्वा च तान् बलात् ॥३-७३-५॥
verses.text:
"On shinning up those trees, or else, even on forcefully pelting those fruits down onto ground, then 
you may pelt over, devouring those ambrosial fruits... [3-73-5b, 6a]

verses.sanskrit: फलानि अमृत कल्पानि भक्षयित्वा गमिष्यथः ।तत् अतिक्रम्य काकुत्स्थ वनम् पुषित पादपम् ॥३-७३-६॥
verses.text:
"On crossing over that parkland, oh, Kakutstha, another parkland with blossomy flowers is there, which 
replicates the heavenly Nandana gardens, and a replica of North-Kuru, an all-endowing province... [3-73-6b, 
7a]

verses.sanskrit: नंदन प्रतिमम् तु अन्यत् कुरवः उत्तरा इव ।सर्व काल फला यत्र पादपा मधुर स्रवाः ॥३-७३-७॥
verses.text:
"Wherein the trees fruit all-season fruits that transude nectar, why fruits alone, in that parkland 
all seasons are coeval, as they will be in Caitraratha, the celestial gardens of Kubera... [3-73-7b, 
8a]

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे च ऋतवः तत्र वने चैत्ररथे यथा ।फल भार नताः तत्र महा विटप धारिणः ॥३-७३-८॥
verses.text:
"In there, the trees bear enormous branches which will be crouching under the weight of their own fruits, 
and everywhere such trees will be shining forth like shiny clouds and shining mountains... [3-73-8b, 
9a]

verses.sanskrit: शोबन्ते सर्वतः तत्र मेघ पर्वत संनिभाः ।तान् आरुह्य अथवा भूमौ पातैत्वा यथा सुखम् ॥३-७३-९॥
verses.text:
"Let Lakshmana present those ambrosial fruits to you either by climbing up those trees to pluck them, 
or else by pelting them to ground, according to his convenience... [3-73-9b-10a]

verses.sanskrit: फलानि अमृत कल्पानि लक्षमणः ते प्रदास्यति ।चङ्क्रमंतौ वरान् शैलान् शैलात् शैलम् वनात् वनम् ॥३-७३-१०॥
verses.text:
"Peregrinating finest places by trekking from mountain to mountain, from forest to forest, oh, valorous 
ones, you will trek to an auspicious lotus-lake, called Pampa... [3-73-10b, 11a]

verses.sanskrit: ततः पुष्करिणीम् वीरौ पंपाम् नाम गमिष्यथः ।अशर्कराम् अविभ्रंशाम् सम तीर्थम् अशैवलाम् ॥३-७३-११॥
verses.text:
"It is un-pebbly, un-slippery, un-weedy thereabouts, oh, Rama, equal are its quaysides and emersed are 
its sandbanks, red and blue lotuses beautify that Pampa Lake... [3-73-11b, 12a]

verses.sanskrit: राम संजात वालूकाम् कमल उत्पल शोभिताम् ।तत्र हंसाः प्लवाः क्रौङ्चाः कुरराः चैव राघव ॥३-७३-१२॥
verses.text:
"There the indwellers of Pampa Lake's waters, oh, Raghava, namely the swans, cranes, Kraunca-s and fish-hawks 
will be letting out peeps in tuneful voice... [3-73-12a, 13b]

verses.sanskrit: वल्गु स्वरा निकूजन्ति पंपा सलिल गोचराः ।न उद्विजन्ते नरान् दृष्ट्वा वधस्य अकोविदाः शुभाः ॥३-७३-१३॥
verses.text:
"Thereabout birds will be unflustered on seeing humans, because they are artless to avoid hunting, because 
none kills them, and you may savour them because those birds will be best and burley, similar to ghee-gobs... 
[3-73-13b, 14a]

verses.sanskrit: घृत पिण्ड उपमान् स्थूलान् तान् द्विजान् भक्षयिष्यथः ।रोहितान् वक्र तुण्डान् च नल मीनान् च राघव ॥३-७३-१४॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama in that Pampa Lake there are best fishes, red-carps, and blunt-snouted small porpoises, and 
a sort of sprats, which are neither scraggy, nor with many fish-bones. Lakshmana will reverentially 
offer them to you on skewering them with arrow, and on broiling them on iron rod of arrow after descaling 
and de-finning them. [3-73-14b, 15, 16a]

verses.sanskrit: पंपायाम् इषुभिः मत्स्यान् तत्र राम वरान् हतान् ।निस्त्वक्पक्षानयसतप्तानकृशान्नैककण्टकान् - यद्वा -निः त्वक् पक्षान् अयस तप्तान् अकृशान् न अनेक कण्टकान् ॥३-७३-१५॥
तव भक्त्या समायुक्तो लक्ष्मणः संप्रदास्यति ।भृशम् तान् खादतो मत्स्यान् पंपायाः पुष्प संचये ॥३-७३-१६॥
पद्म गन्धि शिवम् वारि सुख शीतम् अनामयम् ।उद्धृत्य स तदा अक्लिष्टम् रूप्य स्फटिक सन्निभम् ॥३-७३-१७॥
verses.text:
"While you eat those fishes to satiety, Lakshmana will offer you the water of Pampa Lake, which will 
be in the bunches of flowers of that lake, and which will be lotus-scented, pellucid, comfortably cool, 
shiny like silver and crystal, uncontaminated and that way pristine, by lifting it up that water with 
lotus leaf, making that leaf a stoup-like basin... [3-73-16b, 17, 18a]

verses.sanskrit: अथ पुष्कर पर्णेन लक्ष्मणः पाययिष्यति ।स्थूलान् गिरि गुहा शय्यान् वानरान् वन चारिणः ॥३-७३-१८॥
verses.text:
"Oh, best one among men Rama, at Pampa you will see best shaped Vanara-s who have their bunk bed in 
mountain caves, and who will be coming nearby the lake, greedy as they are for water, and those forest 
movers will be moving about in evening time of the day, bellowing like bulls... oh, Rama, Lakshmana 
will show them to you... [3-73-18b, 19, 20a]

verses.sanskrit: साय आह्ने विचरन् राम दर्शयिष्यति लक्ष्मणः ।अपाम् लोभात उपावृत्तान् वृषभान् इव नर्दतः ॥३-७३-१९॥
रूप अन्वितान् च पंपायाम् द्रक्ष्यसि त्वम् नरोत्तम ।साय अह्ने विचरन् राम विटपीन् माल्य धारिणः ॥३-७३-२०॥
verses.text:
"While roving in the evenings, oh, Rama, should you see the serenely water of Pampa, with her trees 
garlanded with their own blooms and twigs, you will discard your own dolour... [3-73-20b, 21a]

verses.sanskrit: शिव उदकम् च पंपायाम् दृष्ट्वा शोकम् विहास्यसि ।सु मनोभिः चितान् तत्र तिलकान् नक्त मालकान् ॥३-७३-२१॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Raghava, there you will see highly delightful and wide-ranging trees like Tilaka and Naktamaala, 
and fully bloomed red and blue lotuses, as well... [3-73-21b, 22a]

verses.sanskrit: उत्पलानि च फुल्लानि पंकजानि च राघव ।न तानि कश्चित् माल्यानि तत्र आरोपयिता नरः ॥३-७३-२२॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Raghava, there is none someone to inwreathe the garlands of those flowers, and indeed, they neither 
dry up, nor drop down... [3-73-22b, 23a]

verses.sanskrit: न च वै म्लानताम् यान्ति न च शीर्यन्ति राघव ।मतंग शिष्याः तत्र आसन् ऋषयः सुसमाहितः ॥३-७३-२३॥
verses.text:
"Erst there were self-collected sages, the disciples of Sage Matanga, and at one time while they were 
bringing the forest produce as ritual paraphernalia, they were much wearied by its weight and they were 
sweating... and all of those earthbound strings of sweat-drops that quickly dropped from their bodies 
were wafted by air onto trees, and on them they reoccurred as strings of flowers, by the merit of the 
ascesis of those sages... oh, Raghava, since those flowers have emerged from sweat-drops, they are imperishable, 
indeed... [3-73-23b, 24, 25]

verses.sanskrit: तेषाम् भार अभितप्तानाम् वन्यम् आहरताम् गुरोः ।ये प्रपेतुः महीम् तूर्णम् शरीरात् स्वेद बिन्दवः ॥३-७३-२४॥
तानि माल्यानि जातानि मुनीनाम् तपसा तदा ।स्वेद बिन्दु समुत्थानि न विनश्यन्ति राघव ॥३-७३-२५॥
तेषाम् गतानाम् अद्य अपि दृश्यते परिचारिणी ।श्रमणी शबरी नाम काकुत्स्थ चिर जीविनी ॥३-७३-२६॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama of Kakutstha, albeit the passing of those sages through the pearly gates, their maidservant, 
a long-lived one and an anchoress, named Shabari, can be seen there, even today... [3-73-26]

verses.sanskrit: त्वाम् तु धर्मे स्थिता नित्यम् सर्व भूत नमस्कृतम् ।दृष्ट्वा देव उपमम् राम स्वर्ग लोकम् गमिष्यति ॥३-७३-२७॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama, she who always stands by probity will depart to the sphere of heaven, on attending to you, 
for you are the referent to god, and a reverent for one and all... [3-73-27]

verses.sanskrit: ततः तत् राम पंपायाः तीरम् आश्रित्य पश्चिमम् ।आश्रम स्थानम् अतुलम् गुह्यम् काकुत्स्थ पश्यसि ॥३-७३-२८॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama, thereafter on your taking course to that western bank of Pampa Lake, oh, Kakutstha, there 
you will see the unequalled and arcane place of hermitage of sage Matanga... [3-73-28]

verses.sanskrit: न तत्र आक्रमितुम् नागाः शक्नुवन्ति तद् आश्रमे ।ऋषेः तस्य मतंगस्य विधानात् तत् च काननम् ॥३-७३-२९॥
verses.text:
"Sage Matanga so methodised that woodland, where even the elephants are rendered incapable to trespass 
in that hermitage of sage Matanga disturbing its serenity... [3-73-29]

verses.sanskrit: मातंग वनम् इति एव विश्रुतम् रघुनंदन ।तस्मिन् नंदन संकाशे देव अरण्य उपमे वने ॥३-७३-३०॥
verses.text:
"Oh, the legatee of Raghu, that woodland which in simile is like a godly forest, similar to the heavenly 
Nandana gardens, overspread with divers birds is renowned as 'Matanga woodland', and oh, Rama, you will 
take delight rejoicingly in that woodland... [3-73-30, 31a]

verses.sanskrit: नाना विहग संकीर्णे रंस्यसे राम निर्वृतः ।ऋष्यमूकः तु पंपायाः पुरस्तात् पुष्पित द्रुमः ॥३-७३-३१॥
verses.text:
"In front of Pampa there is a bountiful but an unclimbable mountain is there with trees in full blossom, 
which is well-protected by baby elephants, and which in earlier times was purposefully created by Brahma, 
and that alone is Mt. Rishyamuka... [3-73-31b, 32]

verses.sanskrit: सु दुःख आरोहणः च एव शिशु नाग अभिरक्षितः ।उदारो ब्रह्मणा चैव पूर्व काले विनिर्मितः ॥३-७३-३२॥
शयानः पुरुषो राम तस्य शैलस्य मूर्धनि ।यत् स्वप्ने लभते वित्तम् तत् प्रबुद्धो अधिगच्छति ॥३-७३-३३॥
verses.text:
"And should a man sleep on the heights of that mountain, and should he dream of gaining some riches 
in that sleep, he gains that much when he wakes up... [3-73-33]

verses.sanskrit: यः तु एनम् विषम आचारः पाप कर्मा अधिरोहति ।तत्र एव प्रहरन्ति एनम् सुप्तम् आदाय राक्षसाः ॥३-७३-३४॥
verses.text:
"But he who is diabolic in his deportment and devilish in comportment, climbs that and hopes to sleep 
soundly, demons capture him while he is in sleep, and rough him up to death, there itself... [3-73-34]

verses.sanskrit: तत्र अपि शिशु नागानाम् आक्रंदः श्रूयते महान् ।क्रीडताम् राम पंपायाम् मतंग आश्रम वासिनाम् ॥३-७३-३५॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama, there the blaring trumpet-blasts of baby elephants that amuse themselves at Lake Pampa, and 
of those that are the indwellers of Matanga hermitage, even there on Mt. Rishyamuka are audible... [3-73-35]

verses.sanskrit: सिक्ता रुधिर धाराभिः संहत्य परम द्विपाः ।प्रचरन्ति पृथक् कीर्णा मेघ वर्णाः तरस्विनः ॥३-७३-३६॥
verses.text:
"The mighty and massy dark-cloud coloured elephants will be hitting head-on, and with dripping blood 
streams on their jumbo heads and temples, they disperse and move about separately, only to come together 
for yet another round of head-to-head collision... [3-73-36]

verses.sanskrit: ते तत्र पीत्वा पानीयम् विमलम् चारु शोभनम् ।अत्यन्त सुख संस्पर्शम् सर्व गन्ध समन्वितम् ॥३-७३-३७॥
verses.text:
"There the elephants are quenched on drinking water of Pampa Lake, that is daintily clean, and neatly 
clear, springlike for touch, and in which all scents of flowers of that lake are included, and then 
they will be collectively re-entering the forests, for they are forest ranging elephants... [3-73-37, 
38a]

verses.sanskrit: निवृत्ताः सम्विगाहन्ते वनानि वन गोचराः ।ऋक्षाम् च द्विपिनः चैव नील कोमलक प्रभान् ॥३-७३-३८॥
verses.text:
"On your seeing the bears, also thus the tigers and even the exultant sapphire-blue Rurus with black 
stripes there, you will discard your distress... [3-73-38b, 39a]

verses.sanskrit: रुरून् अपेता अपजयान् दृष्ट्वा शोकम् प्रहास्यसि ।राम तस्य तु शैलस्य महती शोभते गुहा ॥३-७३-३९॥
verses.text:
"But oh, Rama, a cavernous cave lidded with a boulder stone outshines on that mountain, and oh, Kakutstha, 
problematic is an entry into it... [3-73-39b, 40a]

verses.sanskrit: शिला पिधाना काकुत्स्थ दुःखम् च अस्याः प्रवेशनम् ।तस्या गुहायाः प्राक् द्वारे महान् शीत उदको ह्रदः ॥३-७३-४०॥
verses.text:
"On its right at its eastern opening a delightful lake with highly coolant waters is there, fringed 
with divers trees and fraught with numerous tubers and fruits... [3-73-40b, 41a]

verses.sanskrit: बहु मूल फलो रम्यो नाना नग समाकुलः ।तस्याम् वसति सुग्रीवः चतुर्भिः सह वानरैः ॥३-७३-४१॥
verses.text:
"Sugreeva dwells in that cave along with four other monkeys, and at times he will be available on the 
peaks of that mountain, even on its bluffs and cliffs, for he is a terrified monkey..." Thus Kabandha 
informed them. [3-73-41b, 42a]

verses.sanskrit: कदाचित् शिखरे तस्य पर्वतस्य अपि तिष्ठते ।कबंधः तु अनुशास्य एवम् तौ उभौ राम लक्ष्मणौ ॥३-७३-४२॥
verses.text:
That impressive Kabandha adorned with divine garlands and abiding in sky scintillating with the grandeur 
of the dazzle of the sun has bidden both the brothers, Rama and Lakshmana thus, awaited a bid adieu 
from them. [3-73-42b, 43a]

verses.sanskrit: स्रग्वी भास्कर वर्ण आभः खे व्यरोचत वीर्यवान् ।तम् तु ख स्थम् महाभागम् कबंधम् राम लक्ष्मणौ ॥३-७३-४३॥
verses.text:
Rama and Lakshmana who started off on their peregrination said this sentence to that highly providential 
Kabandha, who is temporising in the sky in their vicinity, thus as "you may proceed..." [3-73-43b, 44a]

verses.sanskrit: प्रस्थितौ त्वम् व्रजस्व इति वाक्यम् ऊचतुः अन्तिके ।गम्यताम् कार्य सिद्धि अर्थम् इति तौ अब्रवीत् च सः ॥३-७३-४४॥
verses.text:
On getting the orders for departure from those two brothers who are highly gladdened at the information 
given by Kabandha, he that Kabandha who is ready to exit, he further said to them thus, "proceed for 
the purpose of achieving your task..." [3-73-44b, 45]

verses.sanskrit: सुप्रीतौ तौ अनुज्ञाप्य कबंधः प्रस्थितः तदा ॥३-७३-४५॥
स तत् कबंधः प्रतिपद्य रूपम्वृतः श्रिया भास्कर सर्व देहः ।निदर्शयन् रामम् अवेक्ष्य ख स्थःसख्यम् कुरुष्व इति तदा अभ्युवाच ॥३-७३-४६॥
verses.text:
Kabandha who redeemed his divine form has now got an aureole around him, and while irradiance is tamping 
whole of his mien, he moved further up on sky and reappeared. And staying in the sky he pointed out 
at the side of Mt. Rishyamuka, and then he reiterated, "you befriend Sugreeva..."and then he vanished. 
[3-73-46]
